title: Executive Order 12028—Executive Office of the President (Office of Administration)

flags.justification: Sec. 4(b) explicitly states the Director 'shall not be accountable for the program and management responsibilities of units' within the EOP, which remain with each unit's own head — a real, protective limiting clause bounding the consolidation to administrative support only.
A clear, specific reorganization implementing a named Reorganization Plan with defined effective dates.
Multiple related administrative functions (personnel, finance, IT, records, office services) all coherently serve one purpose: administrative consolidation.
An internal administrative-consolidation order, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing administrative-support function, evergreen by nature.
Sec. 5 explicitly excludes functions vested by law in the head of any unit, a real, protective carve-out preserving other units' statutorily-vested authorities.
An internal administrative reorganization with no third-party compliance regime.
Establishes the Office of Administration within the Executive Office of the President, a lasting institutional structure that persisted for over three decades.
Careful, explicit limiting clauses (Secs. 4(b) and 5) avoid overreach into other units' program and statutory authorities.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/12 = 8.3%. Part of President Carter's 1977-78 Executive Office reorganization (Reorganization Plan No. 1 of 1977); the resulting Office of Administration remained part of the EOP structure for decades.
